levelang.title: levelang.app
levelang.description: Translation calibrated to the reader, with level, mode, and mood as parameters. Four languages, depth over breadth.
levelang.longDescription:
Standard translation produces native-speaker output, which is often useless to someone still learning the language. Levelang calibrates its output along three independent axes: level, mode (written or spoken), and mood (casual or formal), producing up to sixteen meaningfully different translations per language. The same engine works both across languages (English to beginner French) and within a single language, where it can rewrite fluent French as beginner French, or turn written French into spoken French.

Four languages are supported in depth rather than breadth: French, German, Italian, and Mandarin. Every combination is validated by an adversarial quality pipeline with automated judges, and nothing ships with known failures. These capabilities are exposed through a mobile app, a web chat client, and an open-source MCP server that makes proficiency-calibrated translation available to AI assistants.

quiz.title: Language Quiz Service
quiz.description: AI-powered French grammar quiz backend with async problem generation
quiz.longDescription:
A backend service for generating AI-powered French grammar quiz problems. Each problem presents one correct sentence alongside incorrect alternatives with targeted grammatical errors—not random mistakes—helping learners practice language features such as conjugation patterns, auxiliary selection, past participle agreement, pronoun placement, and more.

Built on an async generation pattern with Kafka workers, problems are pre-generated and served from a pool using staleness-weighted random selection—ensuring variety while preventing repetition. A compositional prompt system supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Gemini).
